Title: Low Carbon Inducible2/Fatty Acid Desaturase4 locus in C. reinhardtii directs plastid peroxidase location and trans fatty acid production

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CC425; CC4533-derived parental line backgrounds (PL-1, PL-2)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports protection of photosynthetic membranes from reactive oxygen species via recruitment of membrane-associated peroxidase activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast/thylakoid membranes; phosphatidylglycerol of chloroplast membranes for the associated trans fatty acid phenotype
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LCI2 promotes membrane-bound ascorbate peroxidase activity and influences fatty acid composition indirectly; loss of LCI2 decreases membrane APX activity, reduces 18:2 species, and increases sensitivity to ROS-generating conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LCI2 is produced from the same locus as FAD4 by alternative splicing and shares the first two exons with FAD4. 2. LCI2-specific transcripts accumulate much more strongly than FAD4 transcripts, especially at night and under low CO2. 3. Mutants lacking LCI2 show strongly reduced membrane-associated APX activity, whereas fad4-only mutants do not, indicating an LCI2-specific role in peroxidase recruitment. 4. Co-expression assays in E. coli showed LCI2 recruits APX1-GFP to membrane fractions. 5. Loss of LCI2 contributes to altered fatty acid composition and enhanced growth defects/oxidative damage under DCMU-induced ROS stress.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LCI2-like membrane anchor; similarity to C-termini of thylakoid-associated ascorbate peroxidases; membrane APX-like C-terminal region
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks early in the day (0.5–1 h after lights-on); produced at ~10× the rate of FAD4 at night and ~20× early in the night; strongly induced by CO2 limitation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peaks early in the light period; strongly increased under low CO2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Diurnal cycle; low CO2 limitation; mixotrophic growth; DCMU treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cpf1 knockout/editing; insertional mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; peroxidase activity assay in membrane fractions; heterologous co-expression in E. coli with membrane fractionation; fatty acid profiling; growth assays under DCMU; TBARS lipid peroxidation assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (reanalysis of Strenkert et al. 2019; Brueggeman et al. 2012; Fang et al. 2012; SRA accessions not provided in text); ChIP-seq for H3K4me1/H3K4me3 (Strenkert et al. 2022; SRA accessions not provided in text)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Shared native LCI2/FAD4 promoter, inferred constitutive/diurnally regulated endogenous promoter with single TSS
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes reduced growth under DCMU-induced oxidative stress and increased lipid peroxidation; no clear penalty under standard photoautotrophic growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to improve plastid ROS protection by recruiting APX activity to thylakoid membranes, possibly in combination with FAD4 for membrane lipid redox resilience
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Single TSS supported by promoter-associated H3K4me3 enrichment and H3K4me1 depletion at the shared locus
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LCI2 is not the desaturase itself; it is an alternatively spliced product from the same locus as FAD4. The paper proposes LCI2 functions as a membrane anchor recruiting APX1 near FAD4.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS detoxification through ascorbate peroxidase activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: thylakoid/plastid membrane vicinity when recruited by LCI2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: APX1 is the likely stromal ascorbate peroxidase recruited by LCI2 to membranes, thereby contributing to membrane ROS mitigation rather than directly altering nutrient metabolite synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among APX isoforms, APX1 was selected as the likely stromal/chloroplast enzyme recruited by LCI2. 2. APX1-GFP alone showed minimal membrane association in E. coli. 3. Co-expression with LCI2 or LCI2-HA strongly increased APX1-GFP in purified membrane fractions, supporting LCI2-dependent membrane recruitment.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast stroma; recruited to membrane by LCI2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterologous E. coli co-expression assay context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous co-expression with LCI2 in E. coli; GFP fusion; membrane fractionation; dot blot fluorescence imaging
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pDEST14 overexpression constructs in E. coli
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be co-engineered with membrane anchor modules such as LCI2 to enhance plastid membrane ROS detoxification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: APX1 is a mechanistic interactor supporting the LCI2 function, not the primary mutated nutrient-metabolism gene of the study; included because direct recruitment assays were central to the mechanism.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CC425; CC4533-derived parental line backgrounds (PL-1, PL-2)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chloroplast phosphatidylglycerol-specific trans fatty acid formation branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a trans double bond into a 16-carbon fatty acyl chain in chloroplast phosphatidylglycerol to form 16:1Δ3trans
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized thylakoid membrane lipid feature associated with photosynthetic membrane function and protection under oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FAD4 is required for production of 16:1Δ3trans; loss of FAD4 abolishes 16:1Δ3trans, increases 16:0 precursor, increases lipid peroxidation, and reduces growth under DCMU-induced oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD4 is an alternatively spliced product of the LCI2/FAD4 locus containing the conserved lipid desaturase domain. 2. FAD4-specific transcript abundance correlates with 16:1Δ3trans levels during the diurnal cycle. 3. In insertional and CRISPR mutants lacking FAD4, 16:1Δ3trans is absent, demonstrating FAD4 is the functional desaturase responsible for this lipid product. 4. fad4 mutants accumulate more 16:0, consistent with loss of conversion to 16:1Δ3trans. 5. Loss of FAD4 increases lipid oxidation and reduces growth under DCMU stress, linking the product to ROS mitigation in photosynthetic membranes.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipid desaturase domain PF10520; integral membrane desaturase with four transmembrane helices
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks early in the day (0.5–1 h after lights-on); lower than LCI2 but relatively enriched in the light; not strongly induced by low CO2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early light-phase enriched; associated with diurnal increase in 16:1Δ3trans
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Diurnal cycle; low CO2 comparison; mixotrophic growth; DCMU-induced ROS stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Insertional mutant and CRISPR/Cpf1 mutant generation; RT-qPCR; fatty acid methyl ester profiling by GC-FID; growth assays; TBARS lipid peroxidation assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LCI2; APX1 (hypothesized functional proximity, not directly proven as biochemical interaction)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and ChIP-seq reanalysis from published datasets; accession numbers not provided in text
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Shared endogenous LCI2/FAD4 promoter with single TSS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for production of the specialized plastid trans fatty acid 16:1Δ3trans
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering plastid membrane lipid composition and oxidative stress resilience; may be stacked with LCI2/APX modules to couple membrane lipid remodeling and ROS protection
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Shared promoter region marked by H3K4me3 enrichment/H3K4me1 depletion at a single TSS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption elevates lipid peroxidation and impairs growth under DCMU stress, but no obvious photoautotrophic growth defect under standard conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only the FAD4 splice product, not LCI2, is responsible for 16:1Δ3trans synthesis. The paper does not report an EC number.
